chapter_context.summary: Recounts the conspiratorial decree signing by Darius forbidding prayer to anyone but the king, Daniel's persistence in praying three times a day at his open window, his subsequent consignment to the lions' den, and his miraculous preservation by an angel of God.

outline.text: Daniel's High Promotion and the Spiteful Conspirators' Religious Trap
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Daniel's High Promotion and the Spiteful Conspirators' Religious Trap (6:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: Daniel's Open Prayer and the Trap Sprung Against the King's Will
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Daniel's Open Prayer and the Trap Sprung Against the King's Will (6:10-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: Daniel Cast into the Lions' Den and sealed under Stone
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Daniel Cast into the Lions' Den and sealed under Stone (6:16-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: Miraculous Deliverance by the Angel and Slaying of the Conspirators
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Miraculous Deliverance by the Angel and Slaying of the Conspirators (6:19-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: Darius's Universal Decree Revering the God of Daniel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Darius's Universal Decree Revering the God of Daniel (6:25-28), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

key_themes.title: The Sovereignty of God Over Kingdoms
key_themes.description: A central message of Daniel is that God rules in the kingdom of men, setting up and deposing kings according to His eternal purpose.

key_themes.title: Faithfulness under Pressure
key_themes.description: Daniel and his friends demonstrate unwavering allegiance to God's law in the face of compromise, death, and exile.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the faithfulness and courage demonstrated in Chapter 6, how can you stand firm in your convictions when faced with cultural and societal pressures to compromise?
In what ways does the vision of God's ultimate eternal kingdom in this chapter bring hope and stability to your daily life?

cultural_historical.title: Babylonian and Persian Courts
cultural_historical.text: Daniel was trained in the literature and language of the Chaldeans, serving as a high-ranking official under both Babylonian and Medo-Persian administrations, illustrating the historical reality of the Jewish diaspora.
